reservedFeatures.summary: Escaping closure environment support after escape analysis.
Date, timezone, wall-clock, and monotonic-time adapters.
Source-required tagged Haxe dynamic values and operations.
Contained general exception frames after result lowering is ineligible.
Thread-safe exported status and error-detail boundary.
Reachability-selected type and member reflection metadata.
Network address, socket, and error adapters.
Threads, synchronization, thread roots, TLS, and atomics.
Unicode scalar algorithms not feasible as direct or local specialized C.

selectionRoots.description: A reachable compiler-owned allocation whose lifetime is explicit in HxcIR.
Selected transitively when a compiler-admitted runtime feature needs runtime-sized owned storage.

documentation.runtimeRationale: Unknown runtime sizes and allocator identity need one shared ownership and failure contract. The compiler still keeps fixed, bounded, or nonescaping storage direct when it can prove that representation is sufficient.
documentation.directAlternative: Stack storage, fixed arrays, nonescaping spans, and bounded values remain direct C and never request allocation.
documentation.contract: Implements checked size arithmetic, explicit allocator identity, aligned allocation, failure-atomic resize, and move-only allocation owners.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A program-local allocator is preferred when whole-program escape and size facts permit a narrower specialized owner.

features.summary: Hardened allocator ownership and failure contracts with hosted and custom native evidence.

selectionRoots.description: A reachable ordinary Haxe Array length, checked indexing, or push operation.
A reachable ordinary Haxe Array literal for an admitted unboxed element representation.
A reachable ordinary Haxe Array<T> whose length and shared identity are decided at run time.

documentation.runtimeRationale: Ordinary Haxe Array values combine run-time growth with shared mutable identity. A reference-counted container preserves that identity for the admitted acyclic element slice while the compiler keeps fixed arrays and spans direct and runtime-free.
documentation.directAlternative: Compiler-known fixed arrays and nonescaping spans use direct C storage and bounds checks.
documentation.contract: Implements a move-only resizable unboxed buffer with deterministic checked growth, borrow invalidation, and optional typed element lifecycle callbacks.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: Closed element types and bounded capacities should use a program-local specialized helper when that is smaller and equally correct.

features.summary: Resizable contiguous unboxed storage with checked growth and optional typed element lifecycle callbacks.

selectionRoots.description: A reachable admitted Bytes allocation, range, copy, comparison, or mutation operation.
A reachable haxe.io.Bytes value whose contents or identity live at run time.

documentation.runtimeRationale: General Bytes values have run-time size and shared mutable identity. The selected slice owns that storage without treating arbitrary bytes as text or boxed integers.
documentation.directAlternative: Compiler-known immutable byte tables can remain direct const C data when Haxe identity and mutation are unobservable.
documentation.contract: Implements exact-length arbitrary byte buffers, alias-visible mutation, checked copying, and explicit UTF-8 String-to-bytes copying.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A closed bounded buffer can use a program-local specialization when it preserves the same alias and bounds contract.

features.summary: Fixed-length mutable binary storage with checked ranges and shared Haxe identity.

selectionRoots.description: A reachable managed representation may contain a reference cycle that local retain/release ownership cannot reclaim.
A reachable escaping identity-bearing graph cannot use a proven stack, region, or manual lifetime.

documentation.runtimeRationale: General escaping Haxe graphs need stable identity, cyclic reclamation, and exact roots coordinated across functions. One selected backend owns those shared lifetimes without conservatively scanning arbitrary C memory.
documentation.directAlternative: Nonescaping values, static literals, bounded regions, and explicitly manual ownership remain direct and collector-free.
documentation.contract: Implements the selected precise collector backend over immutable type descriptors and the reviewed allocator ABI.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A closed program-local region is preferred when the compiler can prove all identities, escapes, and destruction points.

features.summary: Precise non-moving mark-and-sweep collection with explicit exact roots, pins, pressure thresholds, and reports.

selectionRoots.description: A reachable admitted construction, insertion, or membership operation.
A reachable ordinary Haxe Map<Int, Bool> whose contents and shared identity change at run time.

documentation.runtimeRationale: General run-time keys need mutable shared storage. This first Bool specialization avoids Dynamic values and boxing while leaving unproved IntMap methods unsupported.
documentation.directAlternative: A compiler-known immutable integer lookup table can remain direct const C data when mutation and alias identity are unobservable.
documentation.contract: Preserves ordinary Map<Int, Bool> alias identity and key presence while storing both key and value in their exact C scalar forms.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A closed bounded key range can use a program-local bitset or table when the compiler can prove that range and preserve Map identity.

features.summary: Integer-keyed shared Map<Int, Bool> identity with exact unboxed storage.

selectionRoots.description: Reachable Sys.println with a compiler-owned String literal.
Reachable default trace with compiler-owned literal text and source prefix.

documentation.runtimeRationale: One narrow hosted service centralizes exact-length output, embedded-NUL handling, flushing, and status mapping without pulling in general strings or allocation.
documentation.directAlternative: The literal representation stays direct C, but ISO C has no expression that performs the required hosted write and flush side effect.
documentation.contract: Writes one valid length-delimited Haxe String value plus a newline to hosted stdout and reports write or flush failure explicitly.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: Inlining fwrite/fputc/fflush at each call site would duplicate platform and failure policy rather than specialize semantics.

features.summary: Minimal hosted length-delimited literal output with explicit write and flush failure status.

selectionRoots.description: Selected transitively by the precise collector for managed object allocation and tracing.
A reachable escaping object representation needs exact size, alignment, tracing, or cleanup facts.

documentation.runtimeRationale: Escaping object graphs need one immutable, versioned description of payload layout and exact outgoing references. The descriptor remains separate from reflection names and collector-private mark state.
documentation.directAlternative: Nonescaping classes and values with proven direct lifetimes keep ordinary private C storage and no descriptor.
documentation.contract: Defines the collector-neutral internal descriptor and header contract for reachable managed payload types.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A closed bounded region may use a program-local ownership plan when identity, lifetime, and cleanup are fully proven.

features.summary: Selective immutable object/type descriptors with exact trace and optional finalization dispatch.

selectionRoots.description: Requested only by the independent native runtime ABI smoke fixture.

documentation.runtimeRationale: The linked runtime must answer for its own build, but this evidence helper is not a generated-program semantic dependency.
documentation.directAlternative: Generated C checks HXC_RUNTIME_ABI_MAJOR directly with a structural _Static_assert and needs no query call.
documentation.contract: Returns the packed internal hxrt version for independent native compatibility probes; generated Haxe cannot select this query.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A program-local query would report the compiler's assumption rather than the linked runtime's version.

features.summary: Version query for the internal same-major runtime ABI, used only by native seed evidence.

selectionRoots.description: Selected only when another registered feature needs the shared types or ABI macros.

documentation.runtimeRationale: A single header is the smallest dependency that keeps independently packaged runtime slices ABI-consistent.
documentation.directAlternative: Runtime-free generated C includes the standard headers and direct C types it actually needs, so this feature is omitted.
documentation.contract: Defines the narrow C11/C++ foundation and internal ABI macros shared by every packaged hxrt slice; it owns no allocation, failure, or thread state.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A program-local copy would duplicate the internal ABI and visibility contract in every selected slice.

features.summary: Shared C types, internal ABI version, and visibility/alignment macros for selected runtime slices.

selectionRoots.description: Selected transitively by a slice whose C boundary can fail, currently hosted io or native seeds.

documentation.runtimeRationale: The header is the smallest shared failure vocabulary for separately compiled runtime sources.
documentation.directAlternative: Direct generated C uses ordinary control flow when the complete operation and failure semantics can stay local.
documentation.contract: Defines the closed non-throwing status vocabulary returned by current runtime operations; it allocates nothing and stores no error state.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: Duplicating numeric status values in each program-local helper would make independently packaged feature boundaries disagree.

features.summary: Status value definitions used by selected runtime failure boundaries.

selectionRoots.description: Requested only by independent native seed diagnostics and smoke fixtures.

documentation.runtimeRationale: The helper is shared native evidence, not a fallback selected for generated Haxe.
documentation.directAlternative: Generated code branches on typed status values directly and does not need a name lookup.
documentation.contract: Maps every known hxc_status value to a stable diagnostic name without allocating or retaining state.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A fixture could duplicate the switch, but that would stop testing the runtime's own status vocabulary.

features.summary: Symbolic status-name helper used only by native seed evidence.

selectionRoots.description: Requested only by the string differential and selective-package native fixtures; generated Haxe cannot select it yet.

documentation.runtimeRationale: Arbitrary runtime bytes, scalar indexing, ownership transfer, builders, and CString lifetimes need shared validated machinery when future lowering admits them.
documentation.directAlternative: Known literals, constant concatenation, and statically decidable string facts should remain direct compiler-owned C data.
documentation.contract: Adds owned strings, builders, lossy decoding, concatenation, and explicit borrowed or owned CString conversion above the shared scalar slice.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: Closed call sites should receive specialized local operations when their representation, lifetime, and operation set are statically bounded.

features.summary: Owned UTF-8 construction, mutable building, and explicit CString conversion with native evidence.

selectionRoots.description: Selected transitively when a runtime operation consumes the compiler's direct literal representation.
A reachable immutable Haxe String value backed by compiler-owned literal storage.

documentation.runtimeRationale: The header only shares the three-field call-boundary layout needed by selected consumers such as io.
documentation.directAlternative: The literal bytes and length are emitted directly in generated C; no runtime source or constructor is used.
documentation.contract: Defines the allocation-free private hxc_string view used for compiler-owned valid UTF-8 literal storage, including embedded NUL bytes.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: The carrier itself is already the program-specific representation; cloning its ABI would make runtime consumers incompatible.

features.summary: Immutable valid UTF-8 literal carrier with explicit byte length and no allocation or object dependency.

selectionRoots.description: A reachable ordinary Haxe Map<String, V> whose keys, contents, and shared identity change at run time.
A reachable admitted construction, lookup, membership, insertion, removal, or clear operation.

documentation.runtimeRationale: General mutable maps need run-time key ownership and shared identity. The compiler still chooses one exact value layout per specialization, so the runtime does not introduce Dynamic values or universal boxing.
documentation.directAlternative: A compiler-known immutable lookup table can remain direct const C data when Haxe mutation and alias identity are unobservable.
documentation.contract: Preserves ordinary Map<String, V> alias identity while copying canonical UTF-8 keys and keeping each admitted V specialization exact and unboxed; trivial values copy as bytes, while managed direct records use a complete compiler-generated copy/assign/destroy policy.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A closed, bounded map can use a program-local specialization when it preserves String equality, mutation, missing values, and alias identity.

features.summary: String-keyed shared Haxe Map identity with copied UTF-8 keys and exact unboxed value storage.

selectionRoots.description: A reachable ordinary Haxe String.charAt whose receiver or index is known only at run time.
Selected transitively by a broader String feature that reuses the same validated scalar rules.

documentation.runtimeRationale: Runtime-dependent scalar positions need one shared implementation of ADR 0004 indexing, invalid-input checks, and borrowed slice construction without selecting owned strings or allocation.
documentation.directAlternative: A compiler-known literal and index may fold directly to another literal-backed String view.
documentation.contract: Inspects immutable valid-UTF-8 String views with Unicode-scalar indices and no allocation or retained state.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: One closed program can use a generated local scalar helper, but duplicating the UTF-8 decoder across programs risks semantic drift.

features.summary: Allocation-free valid-UTF-8 validation, Unicode-scalar indexing, slicing, comparison, and hashing.
